name: Miño River and Thermal Springs Trail
name_original: Ourense – vuelta por Castrelo de Miño y Termas de Outariz
description: This difficult 36.7-mile touring cycling route in Ourense takes you along the Miño River, past Roman bridges, and to Termas de Outariz.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Barbadás features routes that navigate the Miño River valley and its surrounding areas in Ourense, Spain. The landscape is characterized by riverine paths, undulating terrain, and proximity to the city of Ourense, offering a mix of natural and urban cycling experiences. Elevation changes are present, with some routes involving significant climbs, while others remain relatively flat. The region provides diverse settings for cycling, from riverside trails to routes exploring thermal areas. Best touring cycling routes around Barbadás The most popular touring cycling route is Miño River and Thermal Springs Trail, a 36.7 miles (59.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the Miño River, passing by thermal springs. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Sanabrés Way (West), a difficult 38.3 miles (61.7 km) path. This section of a historic pilgrimage route offers varied terrain and scenic views. Local touring cyclists also love the Ourense – city centre tour, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail leading through urban landscapes, often completed in about 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Barbadás is defined by river valleys, thermal areas, and undulating countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy city explorations to challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Barbadás are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Barbadás's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Barbadás?
A: There are over 150 touring cycling routes around Barbadás, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The region is well-explored by the komoot community, with more than 1000 touring cyclists having used komoot to discover its varied terrain.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Barbadás?
A: The routes around Barbadás cater to various abilities. While the majority, over 130, are classified as difficult, there are also more than 20 moderate routes and 5 easy routes available, ensuring options for both experienced cyclists and those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Barbadás?
A: Yes, there are easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a less strenuous ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017176550/ourense-city-centre-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ourense – city centre tour&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.8-mile (7.7 km) route that takes you through urban landscapes, typically completed in about 33 minutes.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes around Barbadás?
A: Many routes in the Barbadás area offer access to natural and historical attractions. You can explore thermal areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5017252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz and Burga de Canedo Thermal Baths&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by beautiful waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantiño Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3829291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Boutureira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, you might encounter landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Burgas Roman Baths&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Barbadás?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs – Millennium Bridge loop from Ourense-Empalme&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 52.4 km (32.6 miles) ride. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs – Millennium Bridge loop from Ourense-San Francisco&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 28 km (17.4 miles) loop.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Barbadás?
A: The touring cycling routes around Barbadás are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse settings, from riverside trails along the Miño River to routes exploring thermal areas and the undulating countryside, offering a mix of natural beauty and urban exploration.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Miño River valley and thermal areas?
A: Absolutely. The region is characterized by its riverine paths and thermal springs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017210934/mino-river-and-thermal-springs-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño River and Thermal Springs Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, a difficult 36.7-mile (59.1 km) route that directly follows the Miño River and passes by various thermal springs.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in the Barbadás area?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, the Barbadás area offers challenging routes that cover significant distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017182865/sanabres-way-west" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanabrés Way (West)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 38.3-mile (61.7 km) section of a historic pilgrimage route, providing varied terrain and scenic views for an extended cycling experience.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the cycling routes in Barbadás?
A: Yes, some routes offer scenic viewpoints. For instance, if you're exploring the area around Cachamuíña Reservoir, you might find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cachamuiña Reservoir from Mirador del Monte da Chaira&lt;/a&gt;, which provides panoramic views of the lake and surrounding landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Barbadás?
A: The terrain around Barbadás is quite varied. You can expect a mix of riverine paths along the Miño River, undulating countryside, and some routes with significant climbs. While some paths are relatively flat, others will challenge you with elevation changes, providing diverse cycling experiences.
tips: Recreational park with cafeteria at Club Náutico Castrelo de Miño. Olympic and national level rowing competitions are held in this quiet section of the Miño River.

The church of Santa María de Castrelo de Miño is located on the cliff overlooking the Miño river reservoir, near the town of Ribadavia. Spectacular views of the church and the dam from the new and old bridges that cross the river.

Natural park and path along the river Miño between the town of Laias and the Barbantes hot springs. Cool off by swimming in the cool waters of the river on your adventure. In season, there is a beach bar to buy some food.
Great bridge, funny, reminds me of Tiger and Turtle im Pott🤙
Roman bridge over the river Miño as it passes through Ourense

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: This cycling tour is primarily on paved surfaces, making it well-suited for road touring bicycles. While the broader Ourense region can have unpaved sections, this specific route is mostly smooth, offering a comfortable ride for its duration.

faqs.question: Is this cycling tour suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this tour is rated as difficult and demands a high level of fitness. With approximately 590 meters of elevation gain over 59 kilometers, it's best suited for experienced touring cyclists.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable spots, including the Club Náutico Castrelo de Miño, and a View of Santa María de Castrelo de Miño Church, Ribadavia Bridge, and Miño River Dam. Further along, you can visit the Barbantes Natural Pools, enjoy the Riverside Walk Along the Miño River — Ourense, and cross the impressive Millennium Bridge. Don't miss the View of the Roman Bridge in Ourense.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque landscapes year-round, but spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite warm, especially in the afternoon, while winter might bring cooler, wetter weather.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Ourense, as a starting point, offers various public parking options. For a convenient start, consider parking near the Miño River in Ourense, which provides good access to the route and its riverside sections.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It primarily uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: While the route is on public paths, bringing a dog on a 59 km difficult touring bicycle route might be challenging. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-trained, can keep pace, and is on a leash where required, especially in populated areas or near wildlife. Consider the dog's endurance for such a long distance.

faqs.question: Does this cycling tour connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several local paths. You'll find sections that overlap with trails like the Paseo Fluvial do Miño, Paseo das Ninfas, Paseo Beiramiño, and parts of the Camiño Xabrés (Oeste).

faqs.question: Are there places to refill water or find refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns and near attractions like Termas de Outariz, where you can find cafes and other establishments to refill water or purchase refreshments. Being along the Ribeiro Wine Route also means opportunities to stop at local wineries.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your touring bicycle, appropriate cycling attire for the weather, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Don't forget your swimwear if you plan to enjoy the thermal springs at Termas de Outariz.

faqs.question: Is this a loop cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Ourense – tour around Castrelo de Miño and Termas de Outariz' is designed as a loop, allowing you to start and finish in the same general area, typically Ourense.
